author: @Staedford
text: I do like your battery work; I believe that we need to stop searching for more sources of energy as that allows us to absolve ourselves from the responsibility to be more frugal.  We need to use less and change how and when we use energy, batteries allow us to do that.

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: My odd idea is, why can't we say have tetrateanite as a stator to generate electricity, why would we NEED a battery, as the magnetic charge of the tetrateanite can actually generate electricity, in a sense it is a store of energy as there is latent electricity in its magnetic field that isn't being tapped..? have a rotor spin in a hollowed cup space on/in/over this stator, spinning, levitating with angled serrations and an opposing magnetic field to the stators serrations and flat panes, with SNSNS magnets around the rim of the spinning levitating rotor whose alternative fields are blocked off from the levitating rotor and stator beneath, so that it can generate field in a surrounding coil? The coil is also magnetically blocked off from the fields of the rotor and stators (who are both magnets), but there is some insulating cladding that protects both the coil and the NSNSN on the magnetic rotor so as to have AC generation be possible in the coil. So in this sense the tetrateanite or the magnet itself is the store of energy. I mean how long would it be generating electricity in this way? You could have a variable clutch of some sort to move the coil away from the rotor if you want to stop electric current from running, although it's anybody's guess as to how to stop the frikkin' rotor from stopping in this idea, lol. I'm definitely making a version with foamalite and some small magnets to show my idea, will post on my channel. So please SUBSCRIBE! I'll do it as soon as I have a job again and can afford a little bag of magnets, guys! SUB SUB SUB here we go! There's a nice lad! yeah! Yeah mate, yeah!
